description: xrpl wallet — Full wallet lifecycle management: key generation, BIP39 mnemonics, encrypted keystore (AES-256-GCM + PBKDF2), message/transaction signing and verification

userStories.title: Encrypted keystore utility (encryptKeystore / decryptKeystore)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need encrypt/decrypt helpers for keystore files so all wallet import/export commands share a single, tested implementation.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/utils/keystore.ts exporting: KeystoreFile type, encryptKeystore(seed, password, keyType, address), decryptKeystore(file, password)

KeystoreFile schema: version=1, address, keyType, kdf='pbkdf2', kdfparams={iterations:600000, keylen:32, digest:'sha256', salt:<hex>}, cipher='aes-256-gcm', cipherparams={iv:<hex>, tag:<hex>}, ciphertext:<hex>
ciphertext is AES-256-GCM encryption of the family seed UTF-8 string using PBKDF2-derived key
salt and iv are randomly generated per call using crypto.randomBytes
All crypto uses Node.js built-in crypto module only (no third-party libs)
src/utils/keystore.test.ts: round-trip test encrypts a known seed then decrypts it and asserts equality
src/utils/keystore.test.ts: decryptKeystore throws on wrong password
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: wallet new — Generate a random wallet
userStories.description: As a user, I want to generate a new random XRPL wallet so I have a fresh address and key pair.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/commands/wallet/new.ts and register it under src/commands/wallet/index.ts, which is added to src/commands/index.ts
xrpl wallet new prints: Address, Public Key, Private Key, Seed (one labelled line each)
--key-type secp256k1|ed25519 flag selects algorithm; default is ed25519
--json flag outputs {address, publicKey, privateKey, seed, keyType} as JSON
Alias: xrpl wallet n
E2E test in tests/e2e/wallet/new.test.ts: run xrpl wallet new --json, assert address starts with 'r', seed starts with 's', publicKey and privateKey are non-empty strings
E2E test: run xrpl wallet new --key-type secp256k1 --json, assert keyType === 'secp256k1'
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: wallet new-mnemonic — Generate a BIP39 mnemonic wallet
userStories.description: As a user, I want to generate a wallet from a fresh BIP39 mnemonic so I can back it up as a word phrase.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/commands/wallet/new-mnemonic.ts registered under wallet command group
xrpl wallet new-mnemonic prints: Mnemonic (12 words), Derivation Path, Address, Public Key, Private Key
Default derivation path is m/44'/144'/0'/0/0
--derivation-path <path> overrides the default
--key-type secp256k1|ed25519 flag; default ed25519
--json outputs {mnemonic, derivationPath, address, publicKey, privateKey, keyType}
Alias: xrpl wallet nm
E2E test: run xrpl wallet new-mnemonic --json, assert mnemonic contains exactly 12 words, address starts with 'r'
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: wallet address — Derive XRPL address from key material
userStories.description: As a user, I want to derive the XRPL address from a seed, mnemonic, or private key so I can verify what address a given key controls.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/commands/wallet/address.ts registered under wallet command group
Accepts exactly one of: --seed <seed>, --mnemonic <phrase>, --private-key <hex>
--key-type secp256k1|ed25519 required when --private-key is used; inferred from seed otherwise
Prints the classic XRPL address (r...)
--json outputs {address, publicKey, keyType}
Exits 1 with error to stderr if no key material provided or multiple provided simultaneously
Aliases: xrpl wallet a, xrpl wallet addr
E2E test: run wallet new --json, capture seed, run wallet address --seed <seed>, assert address matches
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: wallet private-key — Derive private key from seed or mnemonic
userStories.description: As a user, I want to extract the raw private key from a seed or mnemonic for use in other tools.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/commands/wallet/private-key.ts registered under wallet command group
Accepts --seed <seed> or --mnemonic <phrase>
--derivation-path supported when using --mnemonic
--key-type secp256k1|ed25519
Prints private key as hex string (ED-prefixed for ed25519, 00-prefixed for secp256k1 per xrpl.js convention)
--json outputs {privateKey, keyType}
Alias: xrpl wallet pk
E2E test: run wallet new --json, capture seed, run wallet private-key --seed <seed>, assert privateKey is a non-empty hex string
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: wallet public-key — Derive public key from key material
userStories.description: As a user, I want to derive the public key from a seed, mnemonic, or private key.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/commands/wallet/public-key.ts registered under wallet command group
Accepts --seed <seed>, --mnemonic <phrase>, or --private-key <hex>
--key-type secp256k1|ed25519
Prints compressed public key as hex string
--json outputs {publicKey, keyType}
Alias: xrpl wallet pubkey
E2E test: run wallet new --json, capture seed and publicKey, run wallet public-key --seed <seed>, assert outputs match
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: wallet import — Import key material into encrypted keystore
userStories.description: As a user, I want to save an existing key into the encrypted local keystore so I can reference it by address in future commands.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/commands/wallet/import.ts registered under wallet command group
xrpl wallet import <seed|mnemonic|private-key> accepts key material as positional arg or stdin when arg is '-'
--key-type required when input is a raw hex private key
--password <pw> skips interactive prompt and prints warning 'Warning: passing passwords via flag is insecure' to stderr
--keystore <dir> overrides default ~/.xrpl/keystore/; XRPL_KEYSTORE env var also respected (flag takes priority)
Writes <address>.json to keystore dir using encryptKeystore from src/utils/keystore.ts
Errors with exit 1 if file already exists unless --force is passed
Prints: Imported account <address> to <filepath>
Alias: xrpl wallet i
E2E test: import a known seed with --password and --keystore pointing to a temp dir, assert <address>.json exists and contains correct address field
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: wallet list — List keystored accounts
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see all accounts I've saved in the keystore.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/commands/wallet/list.ts registered under wallet command group
Reads all *.json files from keystore directory
Prints one address per line
--json outputs a JSON array of address strings
--keystore <dir> / XRPL_KEYSTORE env var respected
Prints '(empty)' when no keystores exist
Alias: xrpl wallet ls
E2E test: import two wallets to a temp keystore dir, run wallet list --keystore <dir>, assert both addresses appear in output
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: wallet remove — Remove a wallet from the keystore
userStories.description: As a user, I want to delete a keystore entry so I can clean up accounts I no longer use.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/commands/wallet/remove.ts registered under wallet command group
xrpl wallet remove <address> deletes <address>.json from keystore directory
Exits 1 with clear error message if address not found in keystore
--keystore <dir> / XRPL_KEYSTORE respected
Prints: Removed <address>
Alias: xrpl wallet rm
E2E test: import a wallet, run wallet remove <address> --keystore <dir>, assert file no longer exists, assert wallet list no longer shows address
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: wallet decrypt-keystore — Decrypt a keystore file to retrieve seed
userStories.description: As a user, I want to decrypt a keystore file to retrieve the raw seed or private key for export.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/commands/wallet/decrypt-keystore.ts registered under wallet command group
xrpl wallet decrypt-keystore <address> looks up <address>.json in keystore directory
--file <path> accepts explicit file path instead of address lookup
--password <pw> skips interactive prompt (with insecure warning to stderr)
Prints the family seed by default
--show-private-key additionally prints the private key hex
--json outputs {address, seed, privateKey, keyType}
--keystore <dir> / XRPL_KEYSTORE respected
Exits 1 with 'Error: wrong password or corrupt keystore' on decryption failure
Alias: xrpl wallet dk
E2E test: import a wallet with known seed, run decrypt-keystore with correct password, assert seed matches
E2E test: run decrypt-keystore with wrong password, assert exit code 1
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: wallet change-password — Re-encrypt keystore with new password
userStories.description: As a user, I want to re-encrypt a keystore file with a new password.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/commands/wallet/change-password.ts registered under wallet command group
xrpl wallet change-password <address> looks up keystore file
--password <current> and --new-password <new> flags for scripting (both print insecure warning to stderr)
Decrypts with old password, re-encrypts with new password, writes to <file>.tmp then renames to <file> atomically
--keystore <dir> / XRPL_KEYSTORE respected
Prints: Password changed for <address>
Exits 1 with clear error on wrong current password
Alias: xrpl wallet cp
E2E test: import wallet, change-password, decrypt-keystore with new password, assert seed unchanged
E2E test: change-password with wrong current password, assert exit code 1
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: wallet sign — Sign a message or XRPL transaction blob
userStories.description: As a user, I want to sign data with a wallet so I can produce verifiable signatures or sign XRPL transactions offline.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/commands/wallet/sign.ts registered under wallet command group
--message <string> mode: UTF-8 encodes the string then signs; --from-hex treats value as already hex
--message mode prints signature as hex
--tx <path-or-inline-json> mode: parses transaction JSON, signs it using xrpl.Wallet, prints tx_blob and hash on separate labelled lines
--tx mode with --json outputs {tx_blob, hash}
Key material: --seed <seed>, --mnemonic <phrase>, or --account <address> (loads from keystore, requires --password)
--key-type secp256k1|ed25519 when using --seed or --mnemonic
Exits 1 if neither --message nor --tx is provided
Alias: xrpl wallet s
E2E test (message): sign 'hello' with a known seed, assert signature is a non-empty hex string, run again and assert same signature (deterministic)
E2E test (tx): sign a minimal Payment tx JSON with a known seed, assert tx_blob is a non-empty hex string that starts with '12'
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: wallet verify — Verify a message or transaction signature
userStories.description: As a user, I want to verify that a signature was produced by the private key corresponding to a given public key or address.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/commands/wallet/verify.ts registered under wallet command group
--message <msg> --signature <hex> --public-key <hex> mode: verifies message signature
--from-hex flag for hex-encoded messages in --message mode
--tx <tx_blob_hex> mode: decodes blob with xrpl.decode(), extracts SigningPubKey and TxnSignature, verifies
Exits 0 and prints '✓ Valid signature' on success
Exits 1 and prints '✗ Invalid signature' on failure (not an unhandled error)
Alias: xrpl wallet v
E2E test: sign a message, verify it with matching public key → assert exit 0 and output contains 'Valid'
E2E test: verify with tampered signature (flip one hex char) → assert exit 1 and output contains 'Invalid'
E2E test: sign a tx, verify the tx_blob → assert exit 0
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
